methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Primoco UAV is a publicly traded defense and security company founded by Ladislav Semetkovský. The broader Defense & Security sector comprises 21 identifiable peers, including 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms such as Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. The company has accumulated €44M+ in orderbook capital.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow comparables. After filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-tier companies within Defense & Security, fewer than three datapoints remained across each filter category. An all-sector median approach was rejected as methodologically unsound given the heterogeneity of peer-set characteristics and the risk of material misrepresentation.

The sparse comparable universe—particularly the absence of closely-matched public or late-stage private peers at Primoco's valuation tier—prevents reliable triangulation via traditional multiples-based approaches. Analysis would require supplementary methods such as discounted cash flow analysis (conditional on orderbook conversion assumptions) or precedent transaction analysis within the narrower defense-tech segment. The current dataset supports only a conclusory statement: valuation determination requires either (1) expansion of comparable company filtration criteria with explicit tier-spread justification, or (2) transition to intrinsic value methodologies anchored to order fulfillment and margin assumptions.
